description_long: This stereo sound system is a large, multi-tiered speaker stack commonly seen at outdoor events or urban gatherings. The structure consists of three main sections, each with multiple circular speaker cones of varying sizes, arranged in a symmetrical, rectangular configuration. The topmost section features two smaller speakers and two circular ports, while the middle and bottom sections house larger woofers and subwoofers, with visible baffle and port openings. The exterior appears to be constructed from dark, matte-finished material, likely wood or MDF, with visible speaker grilles and cutouts. The overall design is robust and utilitarian, optimized for high sound output and durability in public or urban environments. There are no visible control panels, screens, or branding on the exterior surfaces.
description: A large, multi-tiered stereo sound system with stacked speaker cabinets and multiple circular speaker cones, finished in a dark matte material. The design is robust and utilitarian, suitable for urban outdoor use.
description_view_0: Front view showing all speaker cones, ports, and the full stacked configuration.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thin, rectangular profile of the stacked speaker system.
description_view_2: Rear view showing a solid, featureless back with no visible openings or controls.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, mirroring the thin profile seen in view 1.
attribute_speaker_grille_pattern: Circular and rectangular cutouts for speaker cones and ports, with no visible mesh or decorative grille pattern.
